questions.question:
Design a deterministic finite state automaton (using minimum number of states) that recognizes the following language:
L = { w in {0,1}* | w interpreted as a binary number (ignoring leading zeros) is divisible by 5 }.
What is the minimum number of states in this DFA?
questions.to_find: Minimum number of states in the DFA accepting binary strings divisible by 5

understand.plain: We need a DFA that accepts binary strings whose value, when read as a binary number (ignoring leading zeros), is divisible by 5. The standard approach is to track the remainder when divided by 5 as we read digits left to right. Since remainders can be 0, 1, 2, 3, or 4, we need exactly 5 states.

keywords.explain: A DFA where each state represents the current remainder of the number formed so far when divided by the divisor.
keywords.example: For divisibility by 5, states q0..q4 represent remainders 0..4. Reading a bit b from state qi goes to state q_{(2*i + b) mod 5}.

keywords.explain: The DFA with the fewest states that still recognizes the same language; obtained by merging indistinguishable states.
keywords.example: Two states are distinguishable if some string takes one to an accept state and the other to a reject state.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='420' height='180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><circle cx='50' cy='90' r='30' class='hi'/><circle cx='148' cy='90' r='28' class='bg'/><circle cx='240' cy='90' r='28' class='bg'/><circle cx='328' cy='90' r='28' class='bg'/><circle cx='390' cy='90' r='28' class='bg'/><text x='50' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q0</text><text x='148' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q1</text><text x='240' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q2</text><text x='328' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q3</text><text x='390' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q4</text><text x='50' y='140' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>rem=0 (accept)</text><text x='50' y='155' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>start</text><text x='210' y='20' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>5 states for remainders 0..4 mod 5</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Five states q0 to q4 represent remainders 0 to 4 when dividing the binary number by 5. q0 is the start and only accept state.

given.aim: Find the minimum number of states in a DFA that accepts binary strings whose value is divisible by 5.

terms.meaning: Any binary string over alphabet {0,1}
terms.example: 101 represents decimal 5, which is divisible by 5
terms.connects: The DFA must process each bit of w left to right

terms.meaning: The remainder when the current binary number is divided by 5
terms.example: If current value gives remainder 2, we are in state q2
terms.connects: Reading bit b in state qi transitions to state q_{(2*qi + b) mod 5}

terms.meaning: State where the DFA accepts the input; here remainder = 0
terms.connects: Only q0 (remainder 0) is the accept state

given.plan: Model each state as a remainder value mod 5. Transition function is delta(qi, b) = q_{(2*qi + b) mod 5}. Since remainders 0-4 are all reachable and pairwise distinguishable, the minimum DFA has exactly 5 states.

steps.title: Identify states via remainders
steps.apply: If the number formed so far has value V with remainder i = V mod 5, reading bit b gives new value 2V+b with remainder (2i+b) mod 5. States q0..q4 cover all possible remainders.
steps.note: This construction directly gives a 5-state DFA; q0 is start and accept state.

steps.title: Verify all states are necessary (distinguishable)
steps.apply: State qi can be reached: q0 by empty string (value 0), q1 by '1' (value 1), q2 by '10' (value 2), q3 by '11' (value 3), q4 by '100' (value 4). Each pair of states qi, qj (i!=j) is distinguishable because from qi reading the string that makes the total divisible by 5 differs from qj. Hence no two states can be merged.

solution.result: The minimum number of states in the DFA is 5.

questions.formula_note: Remainder-tracking DFA construction: state = current value mod divisor; transition delta(qi, bit b) = q_{(2i+b) mod 5}.

questions.question:
Consider the regular expression (0+1)*1(0+1). The minimum state finite automaton that recognizes the language represented by this regular expression contains:
A. 3 states
B. 5 states
C. n+2 states
D. None of the above
questions.to_find: Minimum number of states in the DFA for the language (0+1)*1(0+1)

understand.plain: The regular expression (0+1)*1(0+1) describes all binary strings whose second-to-last character is 1. Any string of length >= 2 where the second character from the right is '1'. We need to find the minimal DFA for this language.

keywords.explain: For languages defined by the last k characters, the minimal DFA has states tracking what the last k characters were.
keywords.example: For the last-2-char condition, we track 2-character suffixes.

keywords.explain: The number of states in the minimal DFA equals the number of distinct equivalence classes of the Myhill-Nerode relation.
keywords.example: Two strings x, y are equivalent if for all z, xz in L iff yz in L.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='360' height='140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><circle cx='60' cy='70' r='30' class='bg'/><circle cx='180' cy='70' r='30' class='bg'/><circle cx='300' cy='70' r='30' class='hi'/><text x='60' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q0</text><text x='180' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q1</text><text x='300' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q2</text><text x='60' y='115' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>start</text><text x='300' y='115' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>accept</text><text x='180' y='20' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>(0+1)*1(0+1): 3-state min DFA</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Three states: q0 (start), q1 (seen last char is 1), q2 (accept: second-to-last was 1). The DFA tracks whether the second-to-last character is 1.

given.aim: Find the minimum DFA state count for the language L = {w | second-to-last bit of w is 1}.

terms.meaning: Any string of 0s and 1s (including empty)
terms.connects: The prefix before the last two characters

terms.meaning: The suffix: must end with 1 followed by any bit
terms.connects: The second-to-last character must be 1

terms.meaning: Distinct residual languages for each string prefix
terms.example: Strings ending in 0 and ending in 1 lead to different residual languages
terms.connects: The number of classes = minimum DFA states

given.plan: Apply Myhill-Nerode: classify all strings by their last character (or if length < 1). Classes: (1) strings where last char is not '1' or string is too short — need to see 1 then any char; (2) last char is 1 — one more char suffices for acceptance; (3) accepted (second-to-last was 1, just read one more). This yields 3 distinguishable classes.

steps.title: Identify Myhill-Nerode equivalence classes
steps.apply: Class A: strings where second-to-last is not 1 (or length < 2 and last char is 0, or empty). Residual: need a '1' then any bit to accept. Class B: strings whose last character is 1 (but second-to-last is unknown or 0). Residual: reading any one bit accepts. Class C: strings where second-to-last is 1. Residual: these strings already satisfy the suffix condition — reading one more bit accepts immediately (already in accept after reading that bit from class B). Actually the accept state is when we just verified second-to-last=1.
steps.note: More precisely: q0 = initial/last bit is 0 or empty; q1 = last bit is 1; q2 = accept (second-to-last was 1, just read one more bit). Transitions: q0 on 0->q0, on 1->q1; q1 on 0->q2(accept,last-of-pair=0, second was 1), on 1->q2... wait, q2 is accept but we keep going. Let's define: q0=start (last char is 0 or empty), q1=(last char is 1), q2=accept (second-to-last was 1). From q2 on 0->q0, on 1->q1. This gives exactly 3 states.

steps.title: Verify 3 states are necessary and sufficient
steps.apply: q0 and q1 are distinguishable because from q0, reading '10' accepts (10 in L) but from q1, reading '0' also accepts. However q0 on string '0' rejects while q1 on '0' accepts — so they are distinguishable. q2 (after reading '10') vs q1 (after reading '1'): from q2 reading empty string: q2 IS the accept state so they differ from q0. All 3 states are pairwise distinguishable, so minimum is 3.

solution.result: The minimum state DFA has 3 states. Answer: A. 3 states.

questions.formula_note: Myhill-Nerode theorem applied to suffix languages: minimum DFA states = number of distinct right-invariant equivalence classes.

questions.question:
Given an arbitrary non-deterministic finite automaton (NFA) with N states, the maximum number of states in an equivalent minimized DFA is at least:
A. N^2
B. 2^N
C. 2N
D. N!
questions.to_find: Maximum number of states in the minimized DFA equivalent to an N-state NFA

understand.plain: When converting an NFA to a DFA using the subset construction, the DFA can have states corresponding to subsets of NFA states. An N-state NFA can yield up to 2^N subsets, so the worst-case DFA has 2^N states. After minimization, the count can only decrease (or stay the same), so the maximum is 2^N.

keywords.explain: The standard algorithm to convert NFA to DFA: each DFA state represents a set of NFA states reachable simultaneously.
keywords.example: An NFA with states {q0, q1, q2} can produce DFA states like {q0}, {q1,q2}, {q0,q1,q2}, etc.

keywords.explain: The worst-case DFA from an N-state NFA has 2^N states because there are 2^N possible subsets of N states.
keywords.example: For N=3, up to 2^3=8 DFA states.

understand.visual_alt: An N-state NFA converts to a DFA via subset construction. Each DFA state is a subset of NFA states. With N NFA states, there are 2^N possible subsets, giving at most 2^N DFA states.

given.aim: Determine the maximum number of states in the minimized DFA equivalent to an arbitrary N-state NFA.

terms.meaning: Non-deterministic finite automaton having N states
terms.example: NFA with 3 states: q0, q1, q2
terms.connects: Determines the upper bound on equivalent DFA states

terms.meaning: Algorithm converting NFA to DFA by tracking sets of NFA states
terms.example: DFA state {q0,q1} means NFA could be in q0 or q1
terms.connects: Produces at most 2^N DFA states from N NFA states

terms.meaning: DFA with the fewest possible states, obtained by merging indistinguishable states
terms.example: If two states are equivalent, merge them to reduce count
terms.connects: Minimized DFA has at most as many states as the subset-construction DFA

given.plan: The subset construction produces at most 2^N states. Minimization can only reduce this count. Therefore the maximum number of states in the minimized DFA is 2^N. There exist NFAs (e.g., strings ending in 1 at position N from the end) that actually require 2^N states in the minimal DFA.

steps.title: Apply subset construction bound
steps.apply: An NFA with N states has at most 2^N subsets of states (including the empty set). The subset construction creates at most one DFA state per subset, so the DFA has at most 2^N states.
steps.note: This is an upper bound. Not all 2^N subsets need to be reachable.

steps.title: Confirm the bound is tight
steps.apply: There exist families of NFAs for which the equivalent minimal DFA requires exactly 2^N states. For example, the NFA for the language of binary strings where the Nth symbol from the right is 1 has N+1 states but requires 2^N states in any equivalent DFA. Hence the maximum is 2^N.

solution.result: The maximum number of states in an equivalent minimized DFA is 2^N. Answer: B. 2^N.

questions.formula_note: Subset construction (powerset construction): NFA with N states -> DFA with at most 2^N states; this bound is tight.

questions.question:
Consider a DFA over {a, b} accepting all strings which have number of a's divisible by 6 and number of b's divisible by 8. What is the minimum number of states that the DFA will have?
A. 14
B. 48
C. 15
D. 24
questions.to_find: Minimum number of states in DFA accepting strings where #a divisible by 6 AND #b divisible by 8

understand.plain: We need to simultaneously track the number of a's modulo 6 (which has 6 values: 0-5) and the number of b's modulo 8 (which has 8 values: 0-7). Each DFA state must encode both pieces of information, giving 6 * 8 = 48 states. All these states are distinguishable, so the minimum is 48.

keywords.explain: To accept strings satisfying two independent conditions simultaneously, build a DFA whose states are pairs of states from two separate DFAs.
keywords.example: DFA1 with 6 states (for a mod 6) x DFA2 with 8 states (for b mod 8) = 48-state product DFA.

keywords.explain: A DFA tracking count mod k has k states (one per remainder 0..k-1).
keywords.example: '#a divisible by 6' needs a 6-state DFA; '#b divisible by 8' needs an 8-state DFA.

understand.visual_alt: The DFA state is a pair (i, j) where i = count_a mod 6 (0..5) and j = count_b mod 8 (0..7). Total states = 6 x 8 = 48. The unique accept state is (0,0).

given.aim: Find minimum states for a DFA over {a,b} accepting strings where #a = 0 mod 6 AND #b = 0 mod 8.

terms.meaning: Remainder of the count of 'a' symbols when divided by 6
terms.connects: Needs 6 distinct states to track

terms.meaning: Remainder of the count of 'b' symbols when divided by 8
terms.connects: Needs 8 distinct states to track

terms.meaning: DFA whose states are pairs (i,j) tracking both conditions simultaneously
terms.example: State (2,5) means: 2 a's mod 6, 5 b's mod 8
terms.connects: Total states = 6 * 8 = 48

given.plan: Construct product DFA with states (i, j) where i in {0..5} and j in {0..7}. State (i,j) on input 'a' goes to ((i+1) mod 6, j); on input 'b' goes to (i, (j+1) mod 8). Accept state is (0,0). All 48 states are reachable and pairwise distinguishable.

steps.title: Build individual DFAs
steps.apply: DFA_A for '#a divisible by 6': 6 states q_0..q_5, with transitions q_i on 'a' -> q_{(i+1) mod 6}, on 'b' -> q_i. Accept state: q_0. DFA_B for '#b divisible by 8': 8 states r_0..r_7, with transitions r_j on 'b' -> r_{(j+1) mod 8}, on 'a' -> r_j. Accept state: r_0.
steps.note: These two DFAs independently track the two conditions.

steps.title: Form product DFA
steps.apply: Product DFA has states (q_i, r_j) for i in {0..5}, j in {0..7}. Transitions: (q_i, r_j) on 'a' -> (q_{(i+1) mod 6}, r_j); on 'b' -> (q_i, r_{(j+1) mod 8}). Accept state: (q_0, r_0). All 48 states are reachable (e.g., read i a's and j b's to reach (q_i, r_j)). All pairs are distinguishable since qi and qk (i!=k) are distinguishable in DFA_A. So minimum is 48.

solution.result: The minimum number of states in the DFA is 48. Answer: B. 48.

questions.formula_note: Product construction for intersection of regular languages: states = product of individual state counts; here 6 * 8 = 48.

questions.question:
The smallest finite automaton which accepts the language { w | length of w is divisible by 3 } has:
A. 2 states
B. 3 states
C. 4 states
D. 5 states
questions.to_find: Minimum number of states in DFA accepting strings whose length is divisible by 3

understand.plain: We need a DFA accepting all strings (over some alphabet, say {a}) whose length is a multiple of 3. The length mod 3 can be 0, 1, or 2. Each value requires a distinct state, so the minimum DFA has exactly 3 states.

keywords.explain: A DFA tracking input length mod k has k states: one per possible remainder.
keywords.example: For k=3: states for length mod 3 = 0, 1, 2.

keywords.explain: The 3 states form a cycle: q0 -> q1 -> q2 -> q0 -> ..., with q0 being the accept state.
keywords.example: Reading 3 symbols cycles back to q0 (length 3 = 3*1, accepted).

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='320' height='160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><circle cx='80' cy='80' r='32' class='hi'/><circle cx='200' cy='40' r='28' class='bg'/><circle cx='240' cy='120' r='28' class='bg'/><text x='80' y='85' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q0</text><text x='200' y='45' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q1</text><text x='240' y='125' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>q2</text><text x='80' y='130' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>start/accept</text><text x='160' y='155' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>3 states: q0 (len mod 3=0), q1 (=1), q2 (=2)</text><line x1='112' y1='70' x2='174' y2='48' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='222' y1='64' x2='242' y2='95' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='215' y1='143' x2='112' y2='98' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><defs><marker id='arr' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0,0 L0,6 L8,3 z' fill='#7F77DD'/></marker></defs></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Three states q0, q1, q2 arranged in a cycle. q0 is start and accept state (length mod 3 = 0). Each input symbol advances to the next state cyclically.

given.aim: Find the minimum DFA states for L = {w | |w| mod 3 = 0}.

terms.meaning: Length of string w
terms.connects: The DFA tracks length modulo 3

terms.meaning: Remainder when string length is divided by 3
terms.connects: Three possible values (0,1,2) require three distinct states

terms.meaning: Length divisible by 3, i.e., length mod 3 = 0
terms.connects: Only state q0 (remainder 0) is an accept state

given.plan: Create 3 states q0, q1, q2 representing length mod 3 = 0, 1, 2 respectively. Every input symbol transitions qi to q_{(i+1) mod 3}. q0 is start and accept state. Three states are necessary and sufficient.

steps.title: Define states and transitions
steps.formula_raw: delta(qi, a) = q_{(i+1) mod 3} for any symbol a
steps.apply: q0 (length mod 3 = 0): start state, accept state. On any symbol -> q1. q1 (length mod 3 = 1): non-accept. On any symbol -> q2. q2 (length mod 3 = 2): non-accept. On any symbol -> q0. Empty string epsilon has length 0, divisible by 3, so q0 accepts.
steps.note: This DFA correctly accepts all strings of length divisible by 3.

steps.title: Verify minimality
steps.apply: States q0, q1, q2 are pairwise distinguishable: q0 accepts epsilon (length 0); q1 accepts strings of length 2 from q1 (i.e., 'aa' from q1 reaches q0); q2 accepts 'a' from q2. Since all three states behave differently for some input, none can be merged. Minimum is 3 states.

solution.result: The smallest finite automaton has 3 states. Answer: B. 3 states.

questions.formula_note: Modular counting DFA: to accept strings of length divisible by k, use exactly k states forming a cycle.

questions.question:
Consider the regular language L = (111 + 11111)*. The minimum number of states in any DFA accepting this language is:
A. 3
B. 5
C. 8
D. 9
questions.to_find: Minimum number of states in any DFA accepting L = (111 + 11111)*

understand.plain: The language L = (111 + 11111)* consists of all concatenations of blocks of length 3 or 5 ones. This means L contains strings whose length can be expressed as 3i + 5j for non-negative integers i, j. The minimum DFA for this language has 9 states, which can be shown via Myhill-Nerode analysis.

keywords.explain: The set of lengths in (111+11111)* is the numerical semigroup generated by 3 and 5: {0,3,5,6,8,9,10,11,...}. Lengths 1, 2, 4, 7 are NOT achievable.
keywords.example: 3+3=6 ok, 3+5=8 ok, but 4 cannot be written as 3i+5j.

keywords.explain: A non-accepting state from which no accepting state is reachable; absorbs all further transitions.
keywords.example: If we've read '1' and '11' (partial non-decodable prefixes), those may lead to trap states.

understand.visual_alt: The language (111+11111)* accepts strings of length 0, 3, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, ... The minimal DFA needs 9 states to track progress through partial matches and handle the dead state.

given.aim: Find the minimum DFA state count for L = (111 + 11111)*.

terms.meaning: A block of exactly 3 ones
terms.connects: Contributes factor of 3 to achievable lengths

terms.meaning: A block of exactly 5 ones
terms.connects: Contributes factor of 5 to achievable lengths

terms.meaning: Zero or more concatenations of blocks of 3 or 5 ones
terms.connects: The DFA must recognize when accumulated blocks are complete

given.plan: Using Myhill-Nerode theorem, find the number of distinct equivalence classes for strings over {1}. The DFA tracks how many 1s have been read in the current partial block. Since lengths 1 and 2 are dead ends for good reasons (after seeing 1 or 11 we might still extend to 3 or continue; after 1111 might extend to 5), careful analysis gives 9 distinguishable classes: the empty/accepted state, partial reads of 1..7 into the current concatenation, and a dead state.

steps.title: Determine reachable prefix classes
steps.apply: The language is over {1}*. Consider how many 1s remain after the last complete block: a string w is in some equivalence class based on its suffix that hasn't been 'consumed' by a complete 3- or 5-block. States needed: q0 (start/accept, 0 pending), q1 (1 pending '1'), q2 (2 pending '11'), q3 (3 pending '111' - but also accept = just completed a block of 3), q4 (4 pending '1111'), q5 (5 pending '11111' - accept = just completed block of 5), and track combinations. After analysis, 9 states arise: 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7 pending plus a dead/trap state for invalid continuations.
steps.note: Strings of length 1, 2, 4, 7 (mod considerations) that cannot be extended to valid L strings lead to distinguishable states.

steps.title: Count distinguishable classes
steps.apply: The Myhill-Nerode equivalence classes for L = (111+11111)* over {1} are: [epsilon]=[all strings in L with 0 residual], [1], [11], [111]=[strings in L], [1111], [11111]=[strings in L], [111111]=[6 ones, same as two 3-blocks in L], [1111111]=[7 ones, dead - cannot be completed], plus one dead/trap state for lengths 1,2,4,7 that cannot eventually complete to L. A careful analysis (tracking pending ones mod arithmetic with the semigroup {3,5}) yields exactly 9 equivalence classes and hence 9 minimum DFA states.

solution.result: The minimum number of states in any DFA accepting L = (111 + 11111)* is 9. Answer: D. 9.

questions.formula_note: Myhill-Nerode theorem for unary languages defined by numerical semigroups; the number of distinct residual languages determines minimum DFA states.

questions.question:
A minimum state deterministic finite automaton accepting the language
L = { w in {0,1}* | number of 0s and 1s in w are divisible by 3 and 5, respectively }
has:
A. 15 states
B. 11 states
C. 10 states
D. 9 states
questions.to_find: Minimum number of states in DFA where #0s divisible by 3 AND #1s divisible by 5

understand.plain: We need to simultaneously track the count of 0s modulo 3 and the count of 1s modulo 5. There are 3 possible remainders for 0s (0,1,2) and 5 possible remainders for 1s (0,1,2,3,4). The DFA needs to track both, giving 3 * 5 = 15 states in the product DFA, all of which are pairwise distinguishable.

keywords.explain: To accept strings satisfying two independent divisibility conditions, build a product DFA whose states are pairs (i,j) tracking both remainders simultaneously.

keywords.meaning: All 15 states in the product DFA are reachable and distinguishable, so the minimum DFA has exactly 15 states.
keywords.example: State (1,0) is reached by reading one '0' from (0,0); distinguishable from (0,0) since 2 more 0s accept from (1,0) but not from (0,0).

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='380' height='120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='20' y='20' width='340' height='80' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='190' y='50' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>States: (i, j) where i = #0s mod 3, j = #1s mod 5</text><text x='190' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>i in {0,1,2}, j in {0,1,2,3,4}</text><text x='190' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>Total states = 3 x 5 = 15   |   Accept: (0,0)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Product DFA with states (i,j) where i tracks #0s mod 3 (values 0-2) and j tracks #1s mod 5 (values 0-4). Total 15 states; accept state is (0,0).

given.aim: Find minimum DFA states for L = {w | #0s(w) mod 3 = 0 AND #1s(w) mod 5 = 0}.

terms.meaning: Number of 0-symbols in w modulo 3
terms.connects: Needs 3 states to track; independent of #1s

terms.meaning: Number of 1-symbols in w modulo 5
terms.connects: Needs 5 states to track; independent of #0s

terms.meaning: Reading a '0' updates only the #0s-component; reading a '1' updates only the #1s-component
terms.connects: Product DFA correctly models joint condition

given.plan: Build product DFA with 3*5=15 states. All states (i,j) are reachable (read i zeros and j ones from start). All are pairwise distinguishable (states differing in first component can be separated by reading appropriate zeros; states differing in second component by appropriate ones). Minimum DFA has 15 states.

steps.title: Construct product DFA
steps.apply: States are pairs (i,j), i in {0,1,2}, j in {0,1,2,3,4}. Start state: (0,0). Accept state: (0,0). Transitions as above. Total states: 3 * 5 = 15.
steps.note: All 15 states are reachable: state (i,j) is reached from (0,0) by reading i zeros followed by j ones.

steps.title: Verify all states are distinguishable
steps.apply: For states (i1,j1) != (i2,j2): if i1 != i2, read (3-i1) mod 3 zeros then j1 ones from (i1,j1) to reach (0, (j1+j1) mod 5)... more directly: (i1,j1) is distinguished from (i2,j2) by the string of (3-i1) mod 3 zeros followed by (5-j1) mod 5 ones, which leads (i1,j1) to (0,0) (accept) but not (i2,j2) (since i2!=i1 or j2!=j1 means it won't reach (0,0)). Hence all 15 states are necessary. Minimum = 15.

solution.result: The minimum state DFA has 15 states. Answer: A. 15 states.

questions.formula_note: Product construction for simultaneous divisibility constraints: minimum states = product of individual divisors = 3 * 5 = 15.
